title: Gauge Group and Supporting Quirk Index
count_basis: Primary gauge_group only; within a group, each gauge is counted once per canonical quirk.

groups.name: Radial pointer
groups.description: One or more hands sweep an arc or circle against a fixed scale.

supporting_quirks.quirk: mechanical wear, stretch and permanent set
cool-down, heat soak and continued motion
dead time, missed events and duplicate counts
cable, belt, tape and roller errors

groups.name: Segmented display
groups.description: Fixed luminous, gas-discharge, liquid-crystal or material segments form numbers or glyphs.

supporting_quirks.quirk: segment, pixel, lamp or flag failure
cool-down, heat soak and continued motion

groups.name: Liquid column
groups.description: A liquid height, meniscus or liquid/gas boundary moves in a tube, well or gauge glass.

groups.name: Chart or trace recorder
groups.description: A pen, stylus, light beam or scratch records a changing value on a moving medium.

supporting_quirks.quirk: cable, belt, tape and roller errors
dead time, missed events and duplicate counts

groups.name: Dot-matrix or cell array
groups.description: An addressable matrix of dots or cells forms characters, symbols or fields.

supporting_quirks.quirk: segment, pixel, lamp or flag failure

groups.name: Moving tape, ribbon or map
groups.description: A strip, band, tape or printed map travels through a window or past a fixed index.

supporting_quirks.quirk: cable, belt, tape and roller errors
mechanical wear, stretch and permanent set

groups.name: Rotating scan or strobe
groups.description: Timing is converted to apparent position by a rotating scan, flash, glow point or strobe.

supporting_quirks.quirk: multiple echoes, interference and remapped scale
dead time, missed events and duplicate counts

groups.name: Bar, column, wedge or moving-dot display
groups.description: Magnitude is shown as a continuous or discrete bar, wedge, column or advancing dot.

supporting_quirks.quirk: segment, pixel, lamp or flag failure

groups.name: Colour or material-state indicator
groups.description: Colour, opacity, scattering, phase or chemical state changes to indicate value or history.

groups.name: Floating indicator
groups.description: A buoyant float, bob or bulb directly indicates the value by its position.

groups.name: Indicator lamp or illuminated legend
groups.description: A lamp, lens or illuminated legend indicates a state or coarse condition.

supporting_quirks.quirk: segment, pixel, lamp or flag failure
cool-down, heat soak and continued motion

groups.name: Mechanical flag, shutter or semaphore
groups.description: A flag, shutter, vane, striped drum or semaphore changes between visible states.

supporting_quirks.quirk: segment, pixel, lamp or flag failure

groups.name: Rolling drum or counter
groups.description: Numeral drums, cyclometer wheels or register wheels rotate to form a count or value.

supporting_quirks.quirk: mechanical wear, stretch and permanent set

groups.name: Rotating scale or scene
groups.description: A compass card, scale, drum, horizon or scene rotates behind a fixed reference.

groups.name: Flip-element array
groups.description: Bistable discs, dots, tiles or flags physically flip to form symbols or levels.

supporting_quirks.quirk: segment, pixel, lamp or flag failure

groups.name: Free mass, leaf, ball or bubble
groups.description: A freely deflecting mass, leaf, ball, pendulum or bubble is itself the indicator.

groups.name: Linear pointer
groups.description: A pointer, blade, hairline or marker moves along a straight or edgewise scale.

groups.name: Pattern-forming or biological indicator
groups.description: Crystals, living organisms or other emergent patterns provide the indication.

groups.name: Projected spot or shadow
groups.description: A projected light spot, vane shadow or optical image moves across a scale.

groups.name: Resonant or oscillating element
groups.description: A vibrating reed, string or other oscillating element is observed directly.

groups.name: Vector or storage trace
groups.description: A beam or equivalent trace draws waveforms, radar returns or stored vector imagery.

groups.name: Optical null or match
groups.description: The reading is found by visually matching brightness, colour or disappearance at a null.

supporting_quirks.quirk: optical matching, emissivity and observer effects

groups.name: Projected symbology
groups.description: Collimated or head-up symbols are projected into the operator’s field of view.

groups.name: Split-flap display
groups.description: Physical character flaps cycle through intermediate positions to reach the target.
